conflictOfInterest.details: Founding-stage isolation check: the accountable principal VSBDev operates the authoring, review, and finalization agents, so principal-level independence is absent. Agent-level role isolation was strictly maintained: no authoring history, other review output, or adjudication draft was read, and no other EmbeddedKnowledge role script was run during this review execution.

findings.finding: The lesson opener successfully employs a concrete, fictional biological case (Tide-1 protein binding salt-response DNA in a salt-tolerant marsh grass) to raise a compelling question about mechanistic gaps, motivating the learner before introducing technical vocabulary. This passes the Content Standard first-read gate by allowing a college-introductory learner to easily explain what the lesson is about and why it matters after one read.

The phrasing 'activated the correlation–causation prerequisite' and 'activated the models-and-limits prerequisite' refers to internal Premed curriculum course administration names. While descriptive of the concepts, it borders on exposing project structural terminology to the learner.

The sentence 'The table carries the same relationships for a nonvisual or print route' serves as helpful accessibility cueing but reads as technical project meta-commentary rather than seamless narrative.

The worked example for Tide-1 is highly robust, guiding the learner through all standard steps: translation, quality auditing (reliability/relevance), convergence mapping (shared bias/independence), independent checks, and formulation of a calibrated five-part claim.

The two misconception blocks (four votes and randomization boundaries) follow the Content Standard repair sequence: elicitation, neutral incorrect-model statement, disconfirming evidence, repair, and verification.

The Harbor-7 transfer task is a strong, multi-dimensional exercise requiring selection and adaptation of the evidence-integration model in a novel, bounded context (environmental cue C and receptor Harbor-7) with a concrete conflict diagnosis and discriminating study design.

limitations: Expert-simulation review: This review represents a single-pass advisory input by a simulated learning-design expert under standard-lesson-v3 guidelines. No real learner testing was conducted.
Conflict of interest: The same GitHub principal (VSBDev) is responsible for the authoring and review run operators. Agent-level isolation was maintained, but principal-level independence is absent.

Scope restriction: The review is strictly bounded to learning-design metrics (e.g., clarity, coherence, practice, alignment, and transfer). Scientific accuracy, rights audits, and final typesetting checks are excluded.

Technical verification limits: No rendered build environment, screen reader, or keyboard navigation pathways were evaluated physically; findings are based solely on the static Markdown and JSON source files.
